q: A capacitor is used as an electric circuit component to store ___ energy.
The capacitor was originally known as a ___.
A capacitor is an electrical device that stores electrical energy in the form of an ___ ___.
A capacitor consists of two conductors separated by a ___ ___.
An ___ is inserted between its conductors.
___ capacitors are the ones that use plastic film as the dielectric medium.
Film capacitors are available in nearly any value and working voltages up to $1500\text{ V}$.
Film capacitors range from $10\ \%$ to $0.01\ \%$ in ___.
___ capacitors are the ones that use ceramic as the dielectric material.
Ceramic capacitors are used in high-frequency circuits such as audio frequency ($af$) to ___ ___ ($rf$).
Ceramic capacitors are mainly used for high stability performances with ___ ___.
The capacitance values of ceramic capacitors are stable with respect to the applied voltage, frequency and ___.
___ capacitors are the ones that use the oxide layer as the dielectric material.
Electrolytic capacitors have a ___ tolerance capacity.
The two main types of electrolytic capacitors are tantalum and ___.
The failure of electrolytic capacitors can be hazardous, resulting in an ___ or fire.
Electrolytic capacitors are used for filtering and smoothing ___ alternating voltage.
___ capacitors mostly use air as the dielectric medium.
The capacitance of a variable capacitor can be ___ adjusted.
Variable capacitors are used to set the ___ frequency in tuner devices.
Variable capacitors are mostly used in AC circuits especially for high frequency, high power and ___ ___.
A supercapacitor (SC) is also called an ___.
A supercapacitor typically stores $10$ to $100$ times more energy than ___ capacitors.
Supercapacitors tolerate many more charge and discharge cycles than ___ batteries.
Supercapacitors are used for ___ braking in automobiles, buses, and trains.
Smaller units of supercapacitors are used as power backup for ___ ___ ___ ___ (SRAM).
Film capacitors and ceramic capacitors are ___-___ capacitors.
Non-polarized capacitors do not have ___ (positive and negative terminals).
Capacitors are used for blocking ___ current after getting fully charged.
Capacitors allow the ___ current to pass through it.
Capacitors are used to eliminate ___ in automobile ignition systems.
Any flashing light or regular beeping likely uses a ___ capacitor.
The magnitude of the charge on each conductor, $Q$, is called the ___ of the capacitor.
The potential difference between two conductors of the capacitor, $V$, is called the ___ ___ of the capacitor.
The quantity of charge $Q$ on the capacitor is ___ proportional to the potential difference $V$ between the conductors.
The relationship between charge and potential difference is written as $Q = CV$, where $C$ is called ___ of the capacitor.
The capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ___ of the charge to the potential difference of the capacitor.
In the SI system, the unit of capacitance is ___ (F).
If the potential difference of the capacitor is $1\text{ V}$ when it is given a charge $1\text{ C}$, its capacitance is $1\text{ C V}^{-1}$ or $1\text{ F}$.
The unit farad is named in honor of ___ ___.
The farad is a very ___ unit of capacitance.
Typical devices have capacitances ranging from ___ ($\mu\text{F}$) to picofarad ($\text{pF}$).
$1$ microfarad ($\mu\text{F}$) = $10^{-6}\text{ F}$.
$1$ nanofarad (nF) = $10^{-9}\text{ F}$.
$1$ picofarad (pF) = $10^{-12}\text{ F}$.
When the charge on the capacitor is increased, the potential difference also increases ___.
The capacitance of a capacitor remains ___.
The capacitance depends on the size and the ___ of the capacitor.
The capacitance depends on the ___ of the insulator between the two conductors.
The amount of charge given to a conductor to change its potential by one unit is called the ___ ___ of the conductor.
A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two parallel metal plates separated by air or other ___ ___.
___ a capacitor means connecting it to a battery.
___ ___ ___ is defined as charge per unit surface area on each plate.
If the plates are very close together, the ___ ___ between the plates is uniform.
The value of the electric field between the plates can be expressed as $E = \frac{\sigma}{\epsilon}$.
The magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals $Ed$.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to the ___ of its plates.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is inversely proportional to the ___ of the plates.
The permittivity $\epsilon$ of an insulating material is related to that of a vacuum $\epsilon_0$ as $\epsilon = \kappa \epsilon_0$.
The symbol $\kappa$ represents the ___ ___ or the relative permittivity.
The permittivity of vacuum $\epsilon_0 = 8.85 \times 10^{-12}\text{ C}^2\text{ N}^{-1}\text{ m}^{-2}$.
For a vacuum, the dielectric constant $\kappa$ is ___.
For other insulating materials, the dielectric constant $\kappa$ is greater than ___.
The dielectric constant of an insulating material is the ___ of its permittivity to that of vacuum.
The ratio of two capacitances, $C$ and $C_0$, is called the ___ ___ of that insulating material.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected from a battery and air is replaced by an insulating material, the potential difference is found to ___.
Dielectric constant can be expressed as the ratio of ___ ___.
Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ greater than ___.
The dielectric ___ is greater than that of air.
A dielectric provides the advantage of increasing the maximum ___ ___.
A dielectric provides mechanical ___ between the plates.
A dielectric may be able to withstand a larger ___ ___ between the plates than air.
A capacitor stores electrical energy in the form of an ___ ___.
The charging process can be viewed as the apparent ___ of charge between two conductors.
Work is done for the transfer of charge from a conductor at ___ potential to a conductor at higher potential.
The energy stored by the capacitor can be analyzed from the ___ graph.
The work done in charging the capacitor is equal to $\frac{1}{2}QV$ which is the ___ stored by the capacitor.
Before a capacitor is charged, each of its conductors has no charge, and the potential difference between two conductors is ___.
If the average potential difference of the capacitor before and after it is charged is $\overline{V}$, then $\overline{V} = \frac{V}{2}$.
The energy of the capacitor can be expressed as $W = \frac{1}{2}CV^2$.
Placing a ___ in the charging circuit slows down the charging process.
The greater the values of resistance and capacitance, the ___ it takes to charge the capacitor.
The product of the resistance $R$ and the capacitance $C$ is called the ___ ___ $\tau$.
The time constant $\tau$ is measured in ___.
During the charging process, the capacitor voltage $V_C$ ___ with time.
After being charged, the capacitor can be discharged through the ___ ___ $R_L$.
During the discharging process, the voltage of the capacitor ___ ___ with time.
Several capacitors can be connected together to be used in a variety of applications, namely; (i) ___ and (ii) series.
All capacitors connected in parallel have the same ___ ___.
Capacitors in parallel carry different amounts of ___.
The total charge $Q$ on three capacitors in parallel is $Q = Q_1 + Q_2 + Q_3$.
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors connected in parallel is the ___ of the capacitances of the individual capacitors.
For $n$ identical capacitors in parallel, the equivalent capacitance $C_p$ is $nC$.
In a series combination, the negatively charged plate of one capacitor is connected to the ___ charged plate of the other.
Capacitors connected in series each have the same ___.
Capacitors connected in series have different ___ ___.
The total potential difference $V$ of a series combination is $V = V_1 + V_2 + V_3$.
When capacitors are connected in series, the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance is the sum of the ___ of their individual capacitances.
The equivalent capacitance $C_s$ of the series combination of $n$ identical capacitors is $C_s = \frac{C}{n}$.
The single capacitor that replaces a combination is called an ___ ___.
